objective: Shard Kafka DLQ routing instead of sending every failed listener record to partition 0.
rollbackNotes: Preserve unrelated dirty changes; revert only this checkpoint scope if explicitly approved.
nextAction: Continue with next highest-priority READY item: salesforce-parity-research-refresh.
implementationSummary: DeadLetterPublishingRecoverer now routes failed records to kafka.dead-letter using the source record partition instead of hardcoded partition 0.
Shared Kafka DLQ topic definition and local Docker Compose topic initialization now use six partitions.
Kafka tests cover source partition boundary preservation, DLQ multi-partition topic definition, and current source-topic partition compatibility.
validationResults: PASS rg drift check found no fixed partition-0 DLQ routing or kafka.dead-letter:1 Compose definition
residualRisks: Existing local Kafka volumes may retain an older one-partition kafka.dead-letter topic until recreated or altered.
Production uses external Kafka, so release evidence must prove kafka.dead-letter has at least the maximum source topic partition count, retention, and replication policy.
DLQ-specific volume, age, publish-failure, and partition-skew alerting remains a separate observability gap.
Campaign retry scans and audience-resolved job-level keying still need separate scalability work before high-volume claims.
